title: UI-031: drag/drop now uses row position: the upper/main row area moves inside the...
summary: docs-viewer drag/drop could not populate an empty node because "drop inside" was inferred from existing children, so empty intended sections such as Archive behaved only as sibling targets. Outcome: drag/drop now uses row position: the upper/main row area moves inside the target node, while the lower edge moves after it.
body:
## UI Rule Log 2026-04-25 / UI-031

- status: adopted
- route: `/docs/?mode=manage`, `/library/?mode=manage`
- issue: docs-viewer drag/drop could not populate an empty node because "drop inside" was inferred from existing children, so empty intended sections such as Archive behaved only as sibling targets.
- triage: shared docs-viewer interaction refinement
- reasoning: tree editability should not depend on a separate source schema flag or on whether a node already has children. Any doc node can become a parent through the viewer, and loadability/visibility should come from generated doc metadata such as `viewable`.
- outcome: drag/drop now uses row position: the upper/main row area moves inside the target node, while the lower edge moves after it. The index toolbar adds an icon-only one-step Undo for the most recent successful move in the current viewer session.
- files changed:
  - `_includes/docs_viewer_shell.html`
  - `assets/js/docs-viewer.js`
  - `assets/css/main.css`
  - `assets/studio/data/studio_config.json`
  - `_docs/ui-request-docs-viewer-index-drag-undo-task.md`
  - `_docs/docs-viewer-management.md`
  - `_docs/ui-framework.md`
  - `_docs/studio-ui-rules.md`
- local verification:
  - in manage mode, drag a root doc onto empty Archive and confirm it becomes a child
  - use the index Undo action and confirm the doc returns to its prior parent/order
  - drag a doc onto an ordinary node without children and confirm that node gains a child while remaining loadable
- follow-up:
  - consider a more explicit visual drop-zone affordance if row-position targeting proves too subtle in manual use
effect: tree editability should not depend on a separate source schema flag or on whether a node already has children.
verification: in manage mode, drag a root doc onto empty Archive and confirm it becomes a child
use the index Undo action and confirm the doc returns to its prior parent/order
drag a doc onto an ordinary node without children and confirm that node gains a child while remaining loadable
consider a more explicit visual drop-zone affordance if row-position targeting proves too subtle in manual use
